panels.title: Validation F1 — every MLflow run (medi-watch-readmission)
panels.description: Time series of every run that logged a `val_f1`, `test_f1`, or `refit_val_f1` metric. Each model's runs become a separate series so we can see whether re-trains are tracking, regressing, or churning the leaderboard.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT to_timestamp(r.start_time/1000.0) AS "time",
       COALESCE(NULLIF(rt.value, ''), substring(r.run_uuid,1,8)) AS metric,
       m.value AS value
FROM metrics m
JOIN runs r        ON r.run_uuid = m.run_uuid
JOIN experiments e ON e.experiment_id = r.experiment_id
LEFT JOIN tags rt  ON rt.run_uuid = r.run_uuid AND rt.key = 'mlflow.runName'
WHERE e.name = 'medi-watch-readmission'
  AND m.key = 'val_f1'
  AND m.value IS NOT NULL
  AND $__unixEpochFilter(r.start_time/1000)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: @champion model age
panels.description: Wall-clock age of the model version currently tagged `@champion` for `medi-watch-readmission`. A long gap with active drift checks means retrain triggers are firing but candidates are getting rejected by the §10 promotion gate.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - to_timestamp(mv.creation_time / 1000))) / 86400.0 AS age_days
FROM registered_model_aliases rma
JOIN model_versions mv
  ON mv.name = rma.name
 AND mv.version::int = rma.version::int
WHERE rma.name = 'medi-watch-readmission'
  AND rma.alias = 'champion'
ORDER BY mv.creation_time DESC
LIMIT 1

panels.title: Registered versions — medi-watch-readmission
panels.description: Every registered version with its aliases (@champion / @rejected), creation time, and any `rejected_reason` tag set by the §retrain_on_drift gate.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT mv.version::int AS version,
       to_timestamp(mv.creation_time / 1000) AT TIME ZONE 'UTC' AS created,
       COALESCE(STRING_AGG(DISTINCT rma.alias, ',' ORDER BY rma.alias), '') AS aliases,
       MAX(CASE WHEN mvt.key = 'rejected_reason' THEN mvt.value END) AS rejected_reason,
       MAX(CASE WHEN mvt.key = 'test_f1' THEN mvt.value END) AS test_f1
FROM model_versions mv
LEFT JOIN registered_model_aliases rma ON rma.name = mv.name AND rma.version::int = mv.version::int
LEFT JOIN model_version_tags mvt ON mvt.name = mv.name AND mvt.version::int = mv.version::int
WHERE mv.name = 'medi-watch-readmission'
GROUP BY mv.version, mv.creation_time
ORDER BY mv.version DESC
LIMIT 25

panels.title: Inference requests per minute (by model version)
panels.description: Rate of /invocations calls served by the inference API, split by the @champion version that handled each call and request status. Confirms the API is receiving traffic and lets the operator see a version rollover as a series break.

panels.title: Inference latency — p50 / p95 / p99 (by model version)
panels.description: Histogram quantiles over the medi_watch_inference_latency_seconds bucket series. The p95 line is the production-SLO indicator; a spike that does not coincide with a champion swap usually means upstream payload-size growth or downstream slow load.

panels.title: Predicted P(readmit=1) — mean and p95 (by model version)
panels.description: Quantiles over the medi_watch_inference_score histogram. The 0.5 line is the decision threshold for class=1; sustained drift away from the validation-time score distribution is the inference-side counterpart to data drift and a strong retraining signal.

panels.title: Rows scored — last 24h
panels.description: Total prediction rows the inference API has served in the last 24 hours across all versions. The increase() over 24h smooths the deployment-startup spike that prometheus_client's instance-counter reset would otherwise produce.

panels.title: @champion reloads — last 24h
panels.description: Count of /reload calls that rebound the inference API to a new @champion alias in the last 24 hours. Driven by the retrain_on_drift DAG's gate task; a non-zero value here means a model rollover happened in the visible window and corresponds to series breaks on the latency / score panels above.
